mechanism: Estimate latent change in multivariate psychological traits over time via structured equations. Model initial levels and slopes with predictors to partition variance into baseline differences and change trajectories. Compare change patterns across groups via factor loadings constrained for measurement invariance, enabling attribution of observed change to true construct variation rather than measurement artifacts.

Detect congruency effects between observed motor actions and executed responses via reaction time and accuracy measures. Decompose joint effects of multiple simultaneous action observations by testing factorial combinations of stimulus congruency at different spatial locations. Model additive, subtractive, or canceling influences when actions overlap in representation space.

Test for selection bias and p-hacking artifacts via asymmetry in effect-size distributions relative to precision, fitted weight functions estimating selection probability per p-value, tests of excess significance given observed power, and shape analysis of p-value distributions. Combine multiple independent diagnostic methods to triangulate presence of reporting bias.

Compare accuracy and confidence-calibration across age groups and cognitive domains via repeated-measures analysis of variance with group and content factors. Partition performance variance using interaction effects to isolate domain-general versus domain-specific age impacts. Estimate metacognitive ability via receiver-operating-characteristic curves.

Estimate continuous output (perceived age) as linear function of continuous predictor (observer age) via regression and median-split comparisons. Decompose observed variance into main effects and interaction effects across subsampled population segments. Test consistency of association via correlation analysis and multiple analytical partitioning strategies.
